text.title: Heuristic Eval Severity Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Heuristic Eval Severity Rubric. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: The rubric carries exactly 5 levels (0..4) with anchored examples per level; custom levels or shifted scales are rejected.
rule.rationale: Stable 0..4 across teams enables comparison; custom scales make findings incomparable across reports.

rule.statement: Every level carries at least 1 concrete example from prior evals; level definitions without examples are rejected.
rule.rationale: Definitions alone drift; anchors keep raters aligned.

rule.statement: When two raters score the same finding, both ratings are recorded; delta > 1 triggers a calibration discussion before publishing.
rule.rationale: Drift detection catches rubric erosion early; recording deltas builds an audit trail.

rule.statement: Rubric carries a last_reviewed date; rubrics unreviewed for >90 days surface a warning on read.
rule.rationale: Rubrics ossify; periodic review keeps anchors representative of the current product.

rule.statement: Context modifiers (revenue-critical, regulated) belong in the triage protocol, NOT in the rubric — keeping severity and context distinct.
rule.rationale: Bundling context modifiers into severity makes the rubric incomparable across contexts and breaks the triage matrix.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-heuristic-eval-severity-rubric.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
